final_message:
All five functions are implemented in `problems.py` and pass docstring examples plus edge cases:

- **`parse_duration`** — single `re.fullmatch(r"(?:(\d+)h)?(?:(\d+)m)?(?:(\d+)s)?")` enforces h→m→s ordering, rejects duplicates ("1h2h"), missing numbers ("h"), whitespace, negatives, and trailing garbage; raises `ValueError` for empty/non-string input and when all groups are absent. Multi-digit and over-range values work naturally.
- **`luhn_valid`** — strips only spaces/hyphens, returns `False` (never raises) for empty or non-ASCII-digit content (including non-string input), standard double-every-second-digit-from-right checksum.
- **`flatten`** — recursive walk building joined keys with `sep`; dicts recurse (empty dicts emit nothing), non-dict values kept as-is; input untouched.
- **`top_k_frequent`** — counts, sorts by `(-count, word)` so frequency descends and ties break alphabetically; returns `[]` for `k <= 0`, slicing handles `k > #distinct`.
- **`rolling_max`** — monotonic deque of indices with strictly decreasing values; `ValueError` for `window <= 0`, `[]` for `window > len(nums)`. Verified O(n): 2M elements with window 1000 in 0.8s.

No signature or docstring changes, stdlib only (`re`, `collections.deque`), no new modules, no commits.
